26.reason: [Swapping disturbs the sorted order of the remaining elements, causing permutations to be generated in non-lexicographical order. For lexicographical order, the subarray after position m should remain sorted, but swapping with a later element may unsort it.]

For lexicographical order, the subarray after position m should remain sorted, but swapping with a later element may unsort it.]
